rulings.Case Title: VENUS HAPP ET AL VS. LINDSAY BRENNAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 6, 2025, line 1. Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint is CONTINUED to August 22, 2025 to be heard with the demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CONAN LI VS. OMED HAMID ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 6, 2025, line 6. Plaintiff's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005(b) and 1010.6. Additionally, proof of service indicates the moving papers were served electronically, but there is no evidence or indication in record that the pro per defendants expressly consented to electronic service per CCP 1010.6, and defendants contend no such consent has been given. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS WING VS. JOHN JEFF PERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiff'S First Set Of Requests For Admission And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 6, 2025, line 7. Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Admitted Trust of Matters Specified in Plaintiff's First Set of Requests for Admission, and for Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant is DENIED. Defendant has filed a substantially compliant response. Request for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff failed to file proof of service of the discovery at issue with the moving papers, and thus did not show that defendant failed to file a timely response as referenced in CCP 2033.280(c). New evidence filed only in reply which is essential to the relief requested in the motion is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 6, 2025, line 8. Defendants' Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer.
